findings.title: Team fee is never removed from reflected transfers, minting unbacked tokens on every taxed transfer

best_agent_match.title: Team fee is never removed from reflected transfers, minting unbacked tokens on every taxed transfer

findings.title: The Uniswap pair is left reflection-eligible, allowing surplus LP tokens to be skimmed

best_agent_match.title: The Uniswap pair is reward-eligible, allowing anyone to skim reflected tokens from LP

findings.title: Auto-swaps are trivially sandwichable because contract sells use `amountOutMin = 0`

best_agent_match.title: Auto-swaps are trivially sandwichable because the contract market-sells with `amountOutMin = 0`

findings.title: ETH payouts use `.transfer()`, so a reverting team wallet can block auto-swaps and non-buy transfers at the threshold

best_agent_match.title: ETH forwarding via `.transfer()` can DOS auto-swaps and block non-buy transfers at the fee threshold

rejected_candidates.title: Owner Can Exclude Accounts From Fees and Drain Liquidity
rejected_candidates.reason: `setExcludeFromFee()` is an explicit privileged configuration knob. Fee exemption by itself does not create an unauthorized drain path or a distinct protocol bug under a standard owner-trusted threat model.

rejected_candidates.title: Owner Can Change Fee Parameters to 0%
rejected_candidates.reason: False. The external setters enforce `1..25`; `removeAllFee()` is private and only used transiently during fee-exempt transfers.

rejected_candidates.title: Insufficient Access Control on Manual Send Functions
rejected_candidates.reason: `manualSend()` is an intended `onlyOwner` treasury forwarding function, not an access-control mistake.

rejected_candidates.title: No Events Emitted for Critical Parameter Changes
rejected_candidates.reason: Informational only and not a realistic protocol-impact vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Validation for Zero Address in setExcludeFromFee
rejected_candidates.reason: No realistic exploit or protocol harm follows from toggling fee exclusion on `address(0)`.

rejected_candidates.title: Hardcoded Swap Router Address with No Migration Path
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a design tradeoff, not a concrete exploitable vulnerability in the deployed logic.

rejected_candidates.title: receive() Function Accepts Any ETH Without Conditions
rejected_candidates.reason: Accepting ETH is required for swap proceeds and accidental ETH receipts do not create protocol harm on their own.

rejected_candidates.title: Lack of Input Validation in includeAccount
rejected_candidates.reason: No exploit path was substantiated; including an address with zero balance does not create meaningful harm.

rejected_candidates.title: Token Burns Through Deliver Break Reflection Model
rejected_candidates.reason: `deliver()` is a standard reflection-token mechanism where callers voluntarily reduce their own reflected balance to benefit other holders; no protocol-breaking flaw is shown.

rejected_candidates.title: Unlimited Token Mint Through includeAccount
rejected_candidates.reason: False. `excludeAccount()`/`includeAccount()` only convert between reflected and token-denominated bookkeeping and `includeAccount()` explicitly zeroes `_tOwned[account]`; they do not mint tokens.

rejected_candidates.title: Owner Can Disable All Transfers Through maxTxAmount
rejected_candidates.reason: False. `_setMaxTxAmount()` only allows values greater than or equal to `100000000000000e9`, which is above the token's total supply, so the owner cannot lower it to freeze transfers.
